title: Angels We Have Heard on High
author: Traditional French
story: This French carol dates to at least the 18th century. The soaring 'Gloria in excelsis Deo' refrain captures the angels' announcement to the shepherds.

lines: Angels we have heard on high Sweetly singing o'er the plains, And the mountains in reply Echoing their joyous strains
What the gladsome tidings be Which inspire your heav'nly song
Come to Bethlehem and see Christ Whose birth the angels sing
Come, adore on bended knee, Christ the Lord, the newborn King
See Him in a manger laid, Whom the choirs of angels praise
Mary, Joseph, lend your aid, While our hearts in love we raise
